frameworks.name: Default Mode Network
frameworks.one_line: intrinsic network classically deactivated by demanding tasks but here probed for activation

frameworks.name: Working Memory Network
frameworks.one_line: task-positive network engaged during verbal working memory operations

frameworks.name: task-negative vs task-positive anticorrelation
frameworks.one_line: prior framing of DMN-WMN as competing networks

core_question: Do DMN regions activate during the preparation phase of a working memory task, alongside the WMN?
key_assumptions: task preparation and execution phases recruit dissociable network states
DMN and WMN can be identified by canonical core regions (mPFC, PCC vs lateral PFC/parietal)
BOLD activation/deactivation indexes attentional resource allocation
manipulations: verbal working memory task vs baseline
key_findings: core DMN regions (mPFC, PCC) and WMN regions coactivated during preparation phase
WMN activated and DMN deactivated during execution phase
interpretations: DMN-WMN coupling reflects allocation of attentional resources driven by task demands rather than a fixed antagonism
DMN can play an active role in task preparation, not only mind-wandering or rest
